verses.text: Les dernières paroles de David n'ont pas nécessairement été ses véritables dernières paroles (voir **1R 1.16** , **29-30** , **33-35** ; **2.1-9** ) mais la dernière fois qu'il exprimait publiquement son adoration de Dieu en tant que roi (cf. **1S 12.1-25** ). • Le verset indique qu'il était sur le point de prononcer un oracle, une révélation de Dieu ( **2S 23.2** ). • Pour d'autres références à David en tant que musicien et poète, voir **1Ch 23.5 ;** **2Ch 29.26** , **27 ;** **Né 12.36 ;** **Am 6.5** .
David a parlé de Dieu, et ce faisant, Dieu a parlé à travers David.

23:3 The Rock of Israel - The Fountain whence Israel was derived. He that ruleth over men must be just - More literally, מושל באדם צדיק moshel baadam tsaddik, He that ruleth in man is the just one; or, The just one is the ruler among men. Ruling in the fear of God - It is by God's fear that Jesus Christ rules the hearts of all his followers; and he who has not the fear of God before his eyes, can never be a Christian.

23:4 He shall be as the light of the morning - This verse is very obscure, for it does not appear from it who the person is of whom the prophet speaks. As the Messiah seems to be the whole subject of these last words of David, he is probably the person intended. One of Dr. Kennicott's MSS. Supplies the word יהוה Yehovah; and he therefore translates, As the light of the morning ariseth Jehovah (see below) He shall be the Sun of righteousness, bringing salvation in his rays, and shining - illuminating the children of men, with increasing splendor, as long as the sun and moon endure.

En parlant de l'alliance avec sa famille, David renvoie à la prophétie que Nathan avait faite à propos de lui et de sa descendance ( **7.12-16** ). Jésus est le roi éternel qui est le "descendant de David" ( **Mt 1.1** ).

23:7 But the man that shall touch them must be fenced with iron and the staff of a spear,.... To remove these thorns, or sons of Belial, out of the way, or to defend himself against them; or weapons of war must be made use of to conquer and destroy them, according to the sense of Ben Gersom, and which De Dieu follows; a man that meddles with them must expect to be as much hurt and wounded by them, all over the body, as if not only the point or iron head of a spear, but the wood or handle of the spear, were thrust up in him;

Il s'agit d'une liste de noms des guerriers d'élite de David, dont les plus éminents sont mentionnés avec un accomplissement notable. Ces guerriers étaient quelques-uns des "outils de fer" que Dieu a utilisés pour "abattre" les impies ( **23.7** ). • La liste se divise en les Trois et les Trente. Certains érudits hébreux ont suggéré que les Trente sont des "Officiers". Si cela est avéré, alors l'expression les Trois désignait peut-être un poste militaire spécifique.\n\n

Il s'agit d'une liste de noms des guerriers d'élite de David, dont les plus éminents sont mentionnés avec un accomplissement notable. Ces guerriers étaient quelques-uns des "outils de fer" que Dieu a utilisés pour "abattre" les impies ( **23.7** ). • La liste se divise en les Trois et les Trente. Certains érudits hébreux ont suggéré que les Trente sont des "Officiers". Si cela est avéré, alors l'expression les Trois désignait peut-être un poste militaire spécifique.\n\n

23:11 A piece of ground full of lentiles - In 1 Chr 11:13 it is a parcel of ground full of barley. There is probably a mistake of עדשים adashim, lentiles, for שעורים seorim, barley, or vice versa. Some think there were both lentiles and barley in the field, and that a marauding party of the Philistines came to destroy or carry them off, and these worthies defeated the whole, and saved the produce of the field. This is not unlikely.

23:12 But he stood in the midst of the ground,.... The field of lentiles: and defended it; the field, so that the Philistines could not ravage it, and get food and forage from it: and slew the Philistines; made a great slaughter among them, entirely routed them, so that they that escaped his sword were obliged to flee: and the Lord wrought a great victory; to whom the glory of it belonged; a similar fact is ascribed to Eleazar before mentioned in 1 Chr 11:13;
La grotte d'Adoullam était un lieu de refuge pour David contre Saül (voir **1S 22.1** ). • La vallée de Refaïtes était située entre Bethléem et Jérusalem (voir **2S 5.17-25** ).

23:14 And David was then in an hold,.... In a strong hold; the strong hold of Zion, as Josephus (x), or one on a rock near the cave of Adullam, see 1 Chr 11:15, and the garrison of the Philistines was then in Bethlehem; which was about six miles from Jerusalem; the valley of Rephaim lay between that and Bethlehem; so far had they got into the land of Judea, and such footing in it, as to have a garrison so near its metropolis.

Ailleurs, cette expression traduit l'idée d'un désir excessif (voir **Nb 11.4 ;** **Ps 106.14** ). On ne sait pas si le besoin d'eau de David était excessif, mais il l'a certainement conduit à faire des exploits dangereux. • David se souvenait de sa jeunesse à Bethléem. L'emplacement du puits est inconnu.

Ailleurs, cette expression traduit l'idée d'un désir excessif (voir **Nb 11.4 ;** **Ps 106.14** ). On ne sait pas si le besoin d'eau de David était excessif, mais il l'a certainement conduit à faire des exploits dangereux. • David se souvenait de sa jeunesse à Bethléem. L'emplacement du puits est inconnu.

Lorsque David a versé l'eau durement gagnée sur le sol, ce n'était pas de l'ingratitude. Au contraire, pour lui l'eau équivalait au sang des guerriers ; elle représentait la vie que seul Dieu était digne de recevoir ( **Lv 17.10-13 ;** **Dt 12.23-24** ).

Assaël, le neveu de David, a été tué par Abner pendant la guerre entre David et Ichebaal ( **2.18-32** ). L'inclusion d'Assaël ici suggère que certaines parties de ces listes remontent au début du règne de David. • Élanan fils de Dodo ne doit pas être confondu avec Élanan fils de Jaïr ( **21.19 ** ; **1Ch 20.5** ).

23:25 Shammah the Harodite,.... Called Shammah the Harorite in 1 Chr 11:27; by a change of the letters "R" and "D", which is frequent: Elika the Harodite; or who was of Harod, as the Targum; these both were from one place: mention is made of the well of Harod, Jdg 7:1.

23:26 Helez the Paltite,.... Who was of a place called Pater, as the Targum; in 1 Chr 11:27, he is called the Pelonite: Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite; who was of the city of Tekoah, the native place of Amos the prophet, famous for oil, about twelve miles from Jerusalem; See Gill on Amo 1:1.

23:27 Abiezer the Anethothite,.... He was of Anathoth, in the tribe of Benjamin, Jos 21:18, the birthplace of Jeremiah the prophet, Jer 1:1, Mebunnai the Hushathite; the same with Sibbecai, 1 Chr 11:29; this man had two names, and was a descendant of Hushah, who came of Judah, 1 Chr 4:4.

23:28 Zalmon the Ahohite,.... The same with Ilai, 1 Chr 11:29; a descendant of Ahoah, a grandson of Benjamin, 1 Chr 8:4, Maharai the Netophathite, who was of Netophah, a city of the tribe of Judah, mentioned along with Bethlehem, Neh 7:26; a place of this name is spoken of in the Misnah (o), famous for artichokes and olives. (o) Misn. Peah, c. 7. sect. 1. Sheviith, c. 9. sect. 5.

23:29 Heleb the son of Baanah, a Netophathite,.... Called Heled, 1 Chr 11:30, Ittai the son of Ribai out of Gibeah of the children of Benjamin: sometimes called Gibeah of Benjamin, Jdg 20:10, and Gibeah of Saul, 1 Sam 11:4, being a city in the tribe of Benjamin, and the birth place of Saul king of Israel; and this man is distinguished hereby from Ittai the Gittite, 2 Sam 15:19.

23:30 Benaiah the Pirathonite,.... Who was of Pirathon, a city in the tribe of Ephraim, Jdg 12:15. Hiddai of the brooks of Gaash; which perhaps ran by the hill Gaash, and was also in the tribe of Ephraim, Jos 24:30. This man is called Hurai, 1 Chr 11:32.

23:31 Abialbon the Arbathite,.... A native of Betharabah, either in the tribe of Judah, Jos 15:6, or in the tribe of Benjamin, Jos 18:18; he is called Abiel in 1 Chr 11:32, Azmaveth the Barhumite; or Bachurimite, the letters transposed, an inhabitant of Bachurim or Bahurim, a city in the tribe of Benjamin, 2 Sam 16:5.

23:32 Eliahba the Shaalbonite,.... Of Shaalboa or Shaaiabin, a city in the tribe of Dan, Jos 19:42; perhaps the Silbonitis of Josephus (p): of the sons of Jashen, Jonathan; in 1 Chr 11:34, it is, the sons of Hashem the Gizonite: sons are spoken of, though but one, as in Gen 46:23. (p) De Bello Jud. l. 3. c. 3. sect. 3.

23:33 Shammah the Hararite,.... From the mountainous country, as the Targum; the Arabic and Syriac versions say, from the mount of Olives: Ahiam the son of Sharar the Hararite: from the high mountain, as the Targum; in 1 Chr 11:35, he is called the son of Sacar.

23:34 Eliphelet the son of Ahasbai, the son of the Maachathite,.... In 1 Chr 11:35, he is called Eliphal the son of Ur: Eliam the son of Ahithophel the Gilonite; David's counsellor, that went off to Absalom, 2 Sam 15:12; Eliam his son is supposed, by the Jews, to be the father of Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah, 2 Sam 11:3; according to Hillerus (q), he is the same with Ahijah the Pelonite, 1 Chr 11:36. (q) Onomastic. Sacr. p. 906.

23:35 Hezrai the Carmelite,.... Of Mount Carmel; or from Carmela, as the Targum, see 1 Sam 25:2; he is called Hezro, 1 Chr 11:37, Paarai the Arbite; or from Arab, as the Targum, a city in the tribe of Judah, Jos 15:52; according to Hillerus (r), the same with "Naarai the son of Ezbai", in 1 Chr 11:37. (r) Onomastic. Sacr. p. 499.

23:36 Igal the son of Nathan of Zobah,.... kingdom in Syria, 2 Sam 8:3; according to Hillerus (s) the same with Joel, 1 Chr 11:38, Bani the Gadite; who was of the tribe of Gad, as the Targum; in the room of this stands "Mibhar, the son of Haggeri", in 1 Chr 11:38. (s) Onomastic. Sacr. p. 856.

23:37 Zelek the Ammonite,.... Who was so either by birth, and became a proselyte; so the Targum says, he was of the children of Ammon; or is so called, because he had sojourned some time in their land, or had done some exploits against them; unless he was of Chepharhaammonai, a city of the tribe of Benjamin, Jos 18:24, Naharai the Beerothite; native of Beeroth, a city in the same tribe, Jos 18:25, armourbearer to Joab the son of Zeruiah; Joab had ten of them, this perhaps was the chief of them, 2 Sam 18:15;
23:38 Ira an Ithrite, Gareb an Ithrite. These were of Jether, as the Targum, a descendant of Caleb, of the tribe of Judah, 1 Chr 2:50 1 Chr 4:15. 2 Samuel 23:39

Le tout dernier nom mentionné est Urie le Hittite. Son beau-père, Éliam, père de Batchéba, est mentionné plus tôt ( **23.34 ** ; voir **11.3** ). En revanche, le Chroniqueur, qui n'a jamais mentionné les péchés de David contre Batchéba et Urie, a placé le nom d'Urie discrètement au milieu de la liste des vaillants hommes de David ( **1Ch 11.10-47** ). Cet auteur a non seulement décrit les péchés ( **2S 11.1-27** ) mais aussi mis l'accent sur le nom d'Urie en le plaçant à la fin de cette liste. Urie n'était pas un conscrit ordinaire mais un membre loyal de la garde d'élite de David, ce qui rend la trahison de David encore plus grave. • Seuls trente-six noms sont mentionnés, en supposant que "Chamma de Harar" ( **23.11** ) est différent de "Chamma de Harod" ( **23.25** ), et que "Benaya fils de Yoyada" ( **23.20** ) est différent de "Benaya de Piraton" ( **23.30** ). Le trente-septième guerrier était peut-être Joab, mentionné de manière anecdotique ( **23:18** , **37** ) et absent de la liste des noms. De nombreux théologiens disent qu'il est exclu parce qu'il était le chef militaire de David et n'avait pas besoin d'être inclus à la liste. L'omission pourrait également représenter la longue relation conflictuelle de David et Joab, qui a finalement été exécuté lorsque Salomon l'a fait mettre à mort comme David l'avait ordonné sur son lit de mort ( **1R 2.5-6** , **28-34** ).
